problems.title: CUDA: Hello from host
problems.instructions:
Write a small CUDA C++ program (compiled with nvcc) that prints exactly:

Hello from CUDA host code!

Use a regular `main` function and standard `printf`. You do NOT need to launch any kernels for this problem.

{hints: Start with a plain C++ main, include <cstdio>, and call printf with the exact string.}

problems.title: CUDA: Two-line output
problems.instructions:
Print two lines from your CUDA program using standard `printf`:

Line 1: CUDA warmup
Line 2: Ready to crunch

Order and spelling must match exactly.

{hints: Call printf twice, once for each line; remember to end each line with \n.}

problems.title: CUDA: Sum a few constants
problems.instructions:
Inside `main`, compute the sum of three integers a=7, b=5, c=3 and print the result ONLY as a number with a trailing newline:

15

Do all computation on the host; no kernels are required.

{hints: Declare three ints, sum them, and print the result with printf("%d\n", sum).}

problems.title: CUDA: Add two constants
problems.instructions:
Write a host-only CUDA program that defines two integers x=10 and y=32 and prints their sum (42) followed by a newline.

{hints: You can reuse the pattern from the previous problem; the key is getting the correct sum and format.}

problems.title: CUDA: Print an array of ints
problems.instructions:
Create an array of ints with the values {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} and print them on one line separated by spaces, followed by a newline:

1 2 3 4 5

Do this entirely on the host.

{hints: Use a simple for loop over the array and conditionally print a space.}

problems.title: CUDA: Max of three numbers
problems.instructions:
Given three hard-coded integers a=4, b=9, c=2, compute the maximum and print it with a newline.

Expected output:

9

{hints: Use if/else or std::max from <algorithm>; keep everything in main.}

problems.title: CUDA: Average of three floats
problems.instructions:
Define three floats: x=1.0f, y=2.0f, z=3.0f. Compute their average and print it with one decimal place, followed by a newline.

Expected output:

2.0

{hints: Use float for accumulation; printf with a %.1f format.}

problems.title: CUDA: Looping on the host
problems.instructions:
Print the line Hello from CUDA! exactly three times, each on its own line.

{hints: Use a simple for loop in main; no GPU code is necessary.}

tests.expected_stdout:
Hello from CUDA!
Hello from CUDA!
Hello from CUDA!

problems.title: CUDA: Print a compile-time constant
problems.instructions:
Declare a constant integer answer=1337 and print it with a newline.

{hints: Mark it as const int answer = 1337; then printf it.}

problems.title: CUDA: Tagged message
problems.instructions:
Print a message in the form:

[CUDA] Ready

Exactly match the brackets, spacing, and casing.

{hints: Build the entire string in a single printf call.}
